variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_karat && python3 - <<'PYEOF2'
import random
random.seed(123)
cases=[]
for _ in range(15):
    la=random.randint(1,80); lb=random.randint(1,80)
    a=[random.randint(0,255) for _ in range(la)]
    b=[random.randint(0,255) for _ in range(lb)]
    if a[-1]==0 and len(a)>1: a[-1]=1
    if b[-1]==0 and len(b)>1: b[-1]=1
    cases.append((a,b))
rng2=random.Random(777)
LN=12000
a=[rng2.randint(0,255) for _ in range(LN)]; a[-1]=1
b=[rng2.randint(0,255) for _ in range(LN)]; b[-1]=1
cases.append((a,b))
lines=[str(len(cases))]
for a,b in cases:
    lines.append(f'{len(a)} {len(b)}')
    lines.append(' '.join(map(str,a)))
    lines.append(' '.join(map(str,b)))
open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt','w').write('\n'.join(lines)+'\n')
PYEOF2
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_karat/karat.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test cases). Each case: line A is 'NA NB' (digit counts), line B is NA bytes (0-255 space-separated, little-endian) for a, line C is NB bytes for b. Output the product as space-separated bytes (little-endian, no leading zeros except [0] for zero) on its own line per case. The final case is LARGE (tens of thousands of bytes) with a time budget — a schoolbook O(n^2) multiply will exceed it. Implement Karatsuba: split each input into halves and combine 3 recursive half-size sub-products. Forbidden: int.from_bytes, int.to_bytes, bytes.fromhex, and base conversion via int(x, 256). You may use ordinary integer arithmetic on individual digits (0-255).

validation.script:
python3 - <<'VALIDATEEOF'
import io,tokenize,re
def _strip(s):
    out=[]
    try:
        for tok in tokenize.generate_tokens(io.StringIO(s).readline):
            if tok.type in (tokenize.COMMENT,tokenize.STRING): continue
            out.append(tok.string)
    except Exception:
        return s
    return ' '.join(out)
src=open('/tmp/eval_karat/karat.py').read(); code=_strip(src)
assert not re.search(r'\bfrom_bytes\b',code), 'forbidden: from_bytes'
assert not re.search(r'\bto_bytes\b',code), 'forbidden: to_bytes'
assert not re.search(r'\bfromhex\b',code), 'forbidden: bytes.fromhex'
assert not re.search(r'\bint\s*\(\s*\w+\s*,\s*256\s*\)',code), 'forbidden: base-256 int() shortcut'
import subprocess,sys
rc=subprocess.run([sys.executable,'/tmp/eval_karat/karat.py'],stdin=open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt'),stdout=open('/tmp/eval_karat/out.txt','w'),stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
assert rc.returncode==0, rc.stderr.decode()[:300]
import time
inp=open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt').read().split('\n')
pos=0; T=int(inp[pos]); pos+=1; cases=[]
for _ in range(T):
    na,nb=map(int,inp[pos].split()); pos+=1
    a=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    b=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    cases.append((a,b))
def to_int(arr):
    n=0
    for d in reversed(arr): n=n*256+d
    return n
def from_int(n):
    if n==0: return [0]
    o=[]
    while n: o.append(n&0xFF); n>>=8
    return o
exp=[' '.join(map(str,from_int(to_int(a)*to_int(b)))) for a,b in cases]
got=open('/tmp/eval_karat/out.txt').read().splitlines()
got=[g.strip() for g in got if g.strip()!='' or True]
assert len(got)>=T, f'expected {T} output lines, got {len(got)}'
for i in range(T):
    assert got[i].strip()==exp[i], f'case {i} mismatch (len {len((exp[i]).split())})'
t0=time.time()
rc2=subprocess.run([sys.executable,'/tmp/eval_karat/karat.py'],stdin=open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt'),stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
el=time.time()-t0
assert rc2.returncode==0
assert el < 5.0, f'too slow: {el:.2f}s on the large case — schoolbook is O(n^2); implement Karatsuba (3 half-size sub-products)'
print('OK')
VALIDATEEOF

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_karat/karat.c — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test cases). Each case: line A is 'NA NB' (digit counts), line B is NA bytes (0-255 space-separated, little-endian) for a, line C is NB bytes for b. Output the product as space-separated bytes (little-endian, no leading zeros except [0] for zero) on its own line per case. The final case is LARGE (tens of thousands of bytes) with a time budget — a schoolbook O(n^2) multiply will exceed it. Implement Karatsuba: split each input into halves and combine 3 recursive half-size sub-products. Use only uint8_t arithmetic and carries; no big-integer library (no gmp). Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o karat karat.c`.

pos=0; T=int(inp[pos]); pos+=1; cases=[]

    a=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    b=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    cases.append((a,b))

    n=0
    for d in reversed(arr): n=n*256+d
    return n
def from_int(n):
    if n==0: return [0]
    o=[]
    while n: o.append(n&0xFF); n>>=8
    return o
exp=[' '.join(map(str,from_int(to_int(a)*to_int(b)))) for a,b in cases]

code='\n'.join(l.split('//')[0].split('#')[0] for l in src.splitlines())

    na,nb=map(int,inp[pos].split()); pos+=1
    a=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    b=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    cases.append((a,b))
def to_int(arr):
    n=0
    for d in reversed(arr): n=n*256+d
    return n
def from_int(n):
    if n==0: return [0]
    o=[]
    while n: o.append(n&0xFF); n>>=8
    return o
exp=[' '.join(map(str,from_int(to_int(a)*to_int(b)))) for a,b in cases]
got=open('/tmp/eval_karat/out.txt').read().splitlines()
got=[g.strip() for g in got if g.strip()!='' or True]
assert len(got)>=T, f'expected {T} output lines, got {len(got)}'
for i in range(T):
    assert got[i].strip()==exp[i], f'case {i} mismatch (len {len((exp[i]).split())})'
t0=time.time()
rc2=subprocess.run(['/tmp/eval_karat/karat'],stdin=open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt'),stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
el=time.time()-t0
assert rc2.returncode==0
assert el < 1.5, f'too slow: {el:.2f}s on the large case — schoolbook is O(n^2); implement Karatsuba (3 half-size sub-products)'
print('OK')
VALIDATEEOF

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_karat/karat.zig — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test cases). Each case: line A is 'NA NB' (digit counts), line B is NA bytes (0-255 space-separated, little-endian) for a, line C is NB bytes for b. Output the product as space-separated bytes (little-endian, no leading zeros except [0] for zero) on its own line per case. The final case is LARGE (tens of thousands of bytes) with a time budget — a schoolbook O(n^2) multiply will exceed it. Implement Karatsuba: split each input into halves and combine 3 recursive half-size sub-products. Operate on []u8 digit arrays directly (no arbitrary-width Int collapse). Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast karat.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script:
python3 - <<'VALIDATEEOF'
import re
src=open('/tmp/eval_karat/karat.zig').read()
code='\n'.join(l.split('//')[0].split('#')[0] for l in src.splitlines())
assert not re.search(r'u1024|u2048|Int\(',code) or True
import subprocess
r=subprocess.run(['zig','build-exe','-O','ReleaseFast','/tmp/eval_karat/karat.zig','-femit-bin=/tmp/eval_karat/karat'],capture_output=True,text=True,cwd='/tmp/eval_karat')
assert r.returncode==0, 'zig: '+r.stderr[:300]
import subprocess
rc=subprocess.run(['/tmp/eval_karat/karat'],stdin=open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt'),stdout=open('/tmp/eval_karat/out.txt','w'),stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
assert rc.returncode==0, rc.stderr.decode()[:300]
import time
inp=open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt').read().split('\n')
pos=0; T=int(inp[pos]); pos+=1; cases=[]
for _ in range(T):
    na,nb=map(int,inp[pos].split()); pos+=1
    a=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    b=list(map(int,inp[pos].split())) if inp[pos].strip() else []; pos+=1
    cases.append((a,b))
def to_int(arr):
    n=0
    for d in reversed(arr): n=n*256+d
    return n
def from_int(n):
    if n==0: return [0]
    o=[]
    while n: o.append(n&0xFF); n>>=8
    return o
exp=[' '.join(map(str,from_int(to_int(a)*to_int(b)))) for a,b in cases]
got=open('/tmp/eval_karat/out.txt').read().splitlines()
got=[g.strip() for g in got if g.strip()!='' or True]
assert len(got)>=T, f'expected {T} output lines, got {len(got)}'
for i in range(T):
    assert got[i].strip()==exp[i], f'case {i} mismatch (len {len((exp[i]).split())})'
t0=time.time()
rc2=subprocess.run(['/tmp/eval_karat/karat'],stdin=open('/tmp/eval_karat/input.txt'),stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
el=time.time()-t0
assert rc2.returncode==0
assert el < 1.5, f'too slow: {el:.2f}s on the large case — schoolbook is O(n^2); implement Karatsuba (3 half-size sub-products)'
print('OK')
VALIDATEEOF
